zh.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon於大約1340年出生在英國英格蘭。她和Sir Vivian de Standon至少有1個女兒。她去世於大約1429年在她的家鄉享年91歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon于大约1340年出生在英国英格兰。她和Sir Vivian de Standon至少有1个女儿。她去世于大约1429年在她的家乡享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Standon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Standen
meanings: English: habitational name predominantly from Standen in Pendleton (Lancashire) and Standean in Ditchling (Sussex), but also from other places similarly named, including Standen in East Grinstead (Sussex), Standen in Biddenden (Kent), Standen in Benenden (Kent), Upper and Lower Standen in Hawkinge (Kent), Standen (Berkshire, Wiltshire, Isle of Wight), and Standon (Devon, Hampshire, Hertfordshire, Staffordshire). The Lancashire, Hampshire, and Wiltshire placenames, as well as Standean in Ditchling (Sussex), derive from Old English stān ‘stone, rock’ + denu ‘valley’; Standen in East Grinstead (Sussex) derives from Old English stān + denu or denn ‘pasture’; Standen in Benenden and Standen in Biddenden (Kent) derive from Old English stānig ‘stony, rocky’ + denn; Standen in Hawkinge (Kent) derives from Old English stān + denn; the Berkshire, Devon, Hertfordshire, Isle of Wight, and Staffordshire placenames derive from Old English stān + dūn ‘hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon was born about 1340, in England, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 daughter with Sir Vivian de Standon. She died about 1429, in her hometown, at the age of 91.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon est née environ 1340, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Sir Vivian de Standon. Elle est décédée environ 1429, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon wurde um 1340 in England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Vivian de Standon mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1429 im Alter von 91 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon è nata il circa 1340 nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Sir Vivian de Standon. Morì circa 1429, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standonは約1340年にイギリスイギリスで生まれた。彼女はSir Vivian de Standonとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1429年に故郷で91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon(은)는 대략 1340년 영국 잉글랜드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Vivian de Standon(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1429년 그녀의 고향에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.Abt 1340–Abt 1429: tany ho any amin'ny 1340–tany ho any amin'ny 1429

pt.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon nasceu aproximadamente 1340, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Sir Vivian de Standon. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1429, em sua cidade natal, com 91 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel De Standon родилась около 1340 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Sir Vivian de Standon. Она умерла около 1429 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 91 год.
